summary: KOLO CRM Dual Authentication Testing Complete - Bearer Token + Cookie system fully verified. All 11 backend tests PASSED. Frontend login/logout/navigation/task completion all working correctly.
passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/auth/login returns token in response body
Backend: GET /api/auth/me accepts Bearer token in Authorization header
Backend: POST /api/tasks (create task) accepts Bearer token
Backend: POST /api/auth/recover returns error for existing accounts
Backend: Endpoints return 401 with invalid token
Frontend: Login stores token in localStorage (kolo_token)
Frontend: Logout clears token from localStorage
Frontend: Navigation between Today/Leads/Tasks tabs works
Frontend: Prospect creation works with Bearer token auth
Frontend: Task completion works with toast notification
critical_code_review_comments: Dual auth system correctly implemented: get_user_from_session() checks Cookie first, then Bearer token (server.py lines 221-257)
authFetch helper correctly adds Bearer token from localStorage (AppShell.js lines 11-18)
Login stores token via login() function in AuthContext (AuthContext.js lines 61-69)
Logout correctly clears localStorage token (AuthContext.js line 82)
seed_data_creation: Created test tasks and prospects via API during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: The dual authentication system (Cookie + Bearer Token) is fully working. Token is stored in localStorage as 'kolo_token' after login. All API calls use authFetch helper that includes Bearer token. Logout clears the token from localStorage. The /api/auth/recover endpoint is designed for users who paid but didn't complete account creation - it returns an error for existing accounts.

verified_features.login_with_token: POST /api/auth/login returns token in response body
verified_features.frontend_token_storage: Token stored in localStorage as kolo_token
verified_features.frontend_token_usage: authFetch helper adds Authorization: Bearer <token> header
